correction_notes: Timed out — produced no output. Script was too long for a single generation. Claude wrote it directly.
task_prompt:
# RSS Feed for Technique Discovery

**Priority**: normal
**Assignee**: claude-terminal
**Initiator**: Ashish
**Worker**: ollama
**TaskType**: code

---

## Objective

Build an RSS ingestion script that monitors key AI/LLM/dev feeds, summarizes new items with qwen3, and saves them to `knowledge/` — the same format as `youtube_ingest.js`. Keeps agents current on Claude Code releases, Ollama updates, and LLM techniques automatically.

---

## What to Build

### File: `scripts/rss_ingest.js`

CLI script. Usage:
```bash
node scripts/rss_ingest.js            # process all feeds in feeds.json
node scripts/rss_ingest.js --feed "https://example.com/rss"  # single feed
node scripts/rss_ingest.js --limit 5  # max items per feed (default: 3)
```

**Step 1 — Load feed list**
Read `scripts/feeds.json`. If it doesn't exist, create it with these defaults:
```json
[
  { "url": "https://www.anthropic.com/rss.xml", "tags": ["claude", "anthropic", "ai"] },
  { "url": "https://ollama.com/blog/rss", "tags": ["ollama", "local-llm"] },
  { "url": "https://simonwillison.net/atom/everything/", "tags": ["llm", "ai-tools", "techniques"] },
  { "url": "https://www.latent.space/feed", "tags": ["llm", "research", "ai"] }
]
```

**Step 2 — Fetch + parse RSS**
For each feed:
- Fetch with native `fetch()`, 10s timeout
- Parse XML manually (no npm packages) — extract `<item>` or `<entry>` elements
- From each item extract: `<title>`, `<link>`, `<pubDate>` or `<updated>`, `<description>` or `<summary>` or `<content>`
- Strip HTML tags from description
- Skip items already processed (check if `knowledge/rss_<slugified-title>_*.md` exists)

**Step 3 — Summarize with qwen3**
For each new item (up to `--limit` per feed):
```
Summarize this article for an AI developer building agentic systems.

Title: <title>
Content: <description — truncated to 3000 chars>

Output JSON:
{
  "summary": "2-3 sentence overview",
  "key_points": ["point 1", "point 2"],
  "action_items": ["what to try or build based on this"],
  "ares_relevance": "how this applies to ARES or local LLM development",
  "tags": ["tag1", "tag2"]
}
```
Use `format: "json"`, 60s timeout per item.

**Step 4 — Save to `knowledge/`**
Same format as `youtube_ingest.js`. Filename: `rss_<slug>_<YYYY-MM-DD>.md`
Where `slug` = title lowercased, spaces→hyphens, max 40 chars, alphanumeric + hyphens only.

Also save to Firestore `knowledge_base` collection with `type: 'rss'`.

**Step 5 — Console output**
```
📡 RSS Ingest
   Feed: <url> (N new items)
   ✅ <item title> → knowledge/rss_<slug>_<date>.md
   ⏭️  <item title> → already processed, skipping
   🎉 Done — N items ingested
```

---

## Also Create: `scripts/feeds.json`

The default feeds list (as shown in Step 1 above).

## Also Update: `package.json`

Add:
```json
"rss": "node scripts/rss_ingest.js"
```

---

## Constraints

- Native `fetch()` only — no `node-fetch`, no `rss-parser` npm package
- Parse XML with string operations / regex — no `xml2js` or similar
- JavaScript only, no TypeScript
- If a feed URL fails (network error, bad XML) → log warning and continue to next feed
- If qwen3 summarization fails for an item → skip that item, log warning
- Deduplication: check filename existence in `knowledge/` before processing

---

## Output

Write complete file contents to:
`/Users/ashishuzelman/rank-higher-media/ares/agent_outbox/task_rss_feed_complete.md`

Format:
```
# Task Complete: RSS Feed Ingestor

## scripts/rss_ingest.js
[full file content]

## scripts/feeds.json
[full file content]

## package.json additions
[new script line only]

## Summary
[2-3 sentences]
```
